title: Employ the Principle of Least Functionality
description: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities.

checks.name: Unnecessary Windows Features are Disabled
checks.purpose: Verify optional Windows features (e.g., Telnet Client, TFTP Client, SMBv1, PowerShell v2) that are not required are disabled

checks.name: Unnecessary Services are Stopped and Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm non-essential services (e.g., Remote Registry, Print Spooler where not needed, Bluetooth) are stopped and set to Disabled

checks.name: Only Authorized Applications are Installed
checks.purpose: Verify installed applications match the authorized software list and no unauthorized or unnecessary applications are present

checks.name: Only Required Server Roles and Features are Installed
checks.purpose: Verify only roles and features required for the server's documented purpose are installed and all others are removed

checks.name: Unnecessary Services are Stopped and Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm non-essential services are stopped and set to Disabled startup type

checks.name: Only Authorized Applications are Installed
checks.purpose: Verify installed applications match the authorized software list and no unauthorized or unnecessary applications are present

checks.name: Server Core or Minimal Installation is Used Where Applicable
checks.purpose: Verify the server uses Server Core or a minimal installation footprint where feasible to reduce attack surface

checks.name: Unnecessary Packages are Removed
checks.purpose: Verify no unnecessary packages (e.g., X11, games, compilers on production servers) are installed

checks.name: Unnecessary Services are Stopped and Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm non-essential services (e.g., avahi-daemon, cups, rpcbind) are stopped and disabled via systemctl

checks.name: Only Required Network Services are Listening
checks.purpose: Verify only authorized services are bound to network interfaces using ss -tlnp or netstat -tlnp

checks.name: Unnecessary Packages are Removed
checks.purpose: Verify no unnecessary packages are installed on this Debian system

checks.name: Unnecessary Services are Stopped and Disabled
checks.purpose: Confirm non-essential services are stopped and disabled via systemctl on Debian

checks.name: Only Required Network Services are Listening
checks.purpose: Verify only authorized services are bound to network interfaces using ss -tlnp

remediation: Remove unnecessary Windows features and optional components. Disable non-essential services. Uninstall unauthorized or unneeded applications. On Linux, remove unnecessary packages and disable unneeded services. Use Server Core on Windows Server where feasible. Maintain an approved service and application list.
